21.correct: The process to remove a judge of the Supreme Court is known as impeachment (removal by Parliament on grounds of proven misbehaviour or incapacity).

wrong.A: 'Recall' is not the constitutional process for judges.
wrong.B: Judicial review is the courts' power to examine laws, not a removal process.
wrong.D: The President cannot dismiss a judge at will.

21.fact: A judge can be removed only by a special-majority address of both Houses of Parliament (Article 124).

22.correct: The Swaran Singh Committee (1976) recommended the inclusion of Fundamental Duties in the Constitution.

wrong.B: The Rajamannar Committee dealt with Centre-State relations (Tamil Nadu).
wrong.C: The Sarkaria Commission examined Centre-State relations.
wrong.D: The Balwant Rai Mehta Committee recommended Panchayati Raj.

22.fact: Fundamental Duties were added by the 42nd Amendment (1976) as Article 51A.

23.correct: If both the offices of President and Vice-President are vacant, the Chief Justice of India acts as the President.

wrong.A: A state Governor does not act as President.
wrong.C: The Attorney General does not act as President.
wrong.D: The Prime Minister does not act as President.

23.fact: This was the situation when CJI M. Hidayatullah acted as President in 1969.

24.correct: The text 'Raga Tarangini' was written by Pandit Lochana (Locana Kavi).

wrong.D: Pt. Bhatkhande was a modern musicologist who systematised Hindustani thaats.

24.fact: Raga Tarangini is valued for recording many ragas still in use today.

25.correct: The rise and fall of ocean tides is explained by the universal law of gravitation - the gravitational pull of the Moon and the Sun on the oceans.

wrong.A: Nuclear fusion in the Sun is a nuclear process, not gravitational.
wrong.C: Magnetic attraction between poles is magnetism, not gravity.
wrong.D: Electron motion around the nucleus is governed by electromagnetic forces.

25.fact: The Moon's gravity is the main cause of tides, with the Sun contributing too.

26.correct: The mass of an object measures its inertia - its resistance to a change in its state of motion.

wrong.A: Weight is the force of gravity on the object, not the same as mass.
wrong.C: Volume is the space it occupies.
wrong.D: Density is mass per unit volume.

26.fact: Mass is constant everywhere, while weight changes with gravity.

27.correct: The longest day in the Northern Hemisphere occurs at the summer solstice (around 21 June), when the Sun is overhead at the Tropic of Cancer.

wrong.A: The spring equinox has equal day and night.
wrong.B: The autumn equinox has equal day and night.
wrong.C: The winter (southern) solstice gives the shortest day in the north.

27.fact: At the June solstice the North Pole is tilted most towards the Sun.

28.correct: China was the first country to land a rover on the far side of the Moon (Chang'e-4, 2019).

wrong.A: The USA has landed on the near side, not the first far-side rover.
wrong.C: India's Chandrayaan-3 landed near the south pole (near side region), not the first far side.
wrong.D: Russia did not achieve the first far-side rover landing.

28.fact: The far side of the Moon always faces away from Earth, requiring a relay satellite for communication.

29.correct: Abhinav Bindra won India's first individual Olympic gold medal (10 m air rifle) at Beijing 2008.

wrong.A: Vijay Kumar won a silver (2012), not the first individual gold.
wrong.D: Sushil Kumar won wrestling medals but not the first individual gold.

29.fact: Bindra's 2008 gold was India's first individual Olympic gold of any kind.

30.correct: Before 1991, heavy industry faced heavy regulation through licensing, quotas and price controls (the 'Licence Raj').

wrong.A: Agriculture was less affected by industrial licensing.
wrong.B: Consumer/small industry was regulated but heavy industry bore the brunt.
wrong.D: It was not limited to banking.

30.fact: The 1991 reforms abolished industrial licensing for most industries.

31.correct: In T20 cricket, after the powerplay, a maximum of 5 fielders are allowed outside the 30-yard circle.
31.fact: During the T20 powerplay (first 6 overs), only 2 fielders are allowed outside the circle.

32.correct: ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) is responsible for managing satellite remote sensing for environmental studies.

wrong.A: The FSI does forest surveys but does not manage satellites.
wrong.B: The CPCB monitors pollution, not satellites.
wrong.D: The MoEFCC is a ministry, not the satellite-operating agency.

32.fact: ISRO's remote-sensing satellites support agriculture, forestry, water and disaster management.

33.correct: Deccan Airways (along with other private airlines) was nationalised in 1953 under the Air Corporations Act.
33.fact: The 1953 nationalisation created Indian Airlines (domestic) and Air India (international).

34.correct: Kartarpur (the Sikh shrine) is located near the Ravi river (in Pakistan, close to the Indian border).

wrong.A: It is not near the Ganga.
wrong.C: It is not near the Yamuna.
wrong.D: It is not near the Sutlej.

34.fact: The Kartarpur Corridor connects the shrine to Indian pilgrims across the border.

35.correct: In December 2024, ICAR-CIFA developed the 'Rangeen Machhli' (ornamental fish) app.

wrong.A: 'Matsya Mitra' is not the app developed here.

35.fact: CIFA (Bhubaneswar) is the Central Institute of Freshwater Aquaculture.

36.correct: The phenomenon described - the regular twice-daily rise and fall of ocean water due to the gravitational pull of the Sun and Moon - is the tide (jwar-bhata).

wrong.A: A tsunami is a giant wave caused by undersea earthquakes.
wrong.B: A cyclone is a storm system.
wrong.D: A western disturbance is a weather system bringing winter rain to north India.

36.fact: High and low tides generally occur twice each day at most coasts.

37.correct: Only statement I is correct - India's first modern census was conducted between 1865 and 1872; statement II about the next census timing is not accurate as stated, so 'Only I'.

wrong.B: 'Only II' is wrong since statement I is correct.
wrong.C: 'Both' is wrong because II is not correct as stated.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong because I is correct.

37.fact: The first synchronous (simultaneous) census of India was held in 1881.

38.correct: 'Bayalata' is a folk theatre/dance form of Karnataka.
38.fact: Bayalata ('open-air play') is an umbrella term for Karnataka's rural theatre traditions.

39.correct: 'Ghumura' is the most popular traditional dance of the tribes of the Kalahandi district of Odisha.

wrong.C: This option does not name the Kalahandi dance.
wrong.D: Singhi Chham is a Sikkim masked dance.

39.fact: The Ghumura dance uses a distinctive drum tied to the dancer's body.

40.correct: Milkha Singh won gold at the Asian Games in 1958 (and again later); 1958 was a landmark year for him, including Commonwealth gold.
40.fact: Milkha Singh, the 'Flying Sikh', narrowly missed a medal at the 1960 Rome Olympics.
